name: Autonomyx Structured Data Parser
description: Automated Python parser code generation from any data sample. Input: raw data (JSON, CSV, XML, YAML, log, plain text, paste, mixed). Output: structured JSON with ready-to-run Python parser code, detected schema, field mapping, and test cases. Routes to Qwen2.5-Coder-32B. For interactive code generation with iteration, use structured-data-parser skill in Claude.

sample_data.placeholder: Paste your raw data here — JSON, CSV, XML, YAML, log lines, plain text, mixed, anything

input_format.placeholder: json / csv / xml / yaml / toml / log / text / mixed / auto-detect

extraction_goal.placeholder: e.g. extract all invoice numbers and amounts, get user emails, parse error codes from logs

node.description: Raw data sample and parsing requirements

model_name.info: Qwen2.5-Coder-32B — best open code model (HumanEval+ 92.7%). Always used for code generation.

temperature.info: Low temperature for deterministic, correct code

node.description: Local code model — data never leaves your VPS
JSON containing ready-to-run parser code, schema, tests

important_notes: Always routes to Qwen2.5-Coder-32B — best open code model for parser generation
Sample data stays on your VPS — never sent to cloud
For PDF/Word/HTML inputs: generated code uses unstructured library
For structured inputs: uses standard Python libraries (json, csv, xml, yaml)
Generated code includes type hints, docstring, error handling, tests
For iterative refinement and multi-turn generation, use structured-data-parser skill in Claude

api_example.curl:
curl -X POST https://flows.openautonomyx.com/api/v1/run/{flow_id} \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer lf-your-api-key' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"sample_data": "{\\"invoice_id\\": \\"INV-001\\", \\"amount\\": 5000, \\"vendor\\": \\"Acme Ltd\\"}", "extraction_goal": "extract invoice_id and amount into a list of dicts"}'

detected_schema.description: Invoice records with ID, amount, and vendor name

response_shape.parser_code:
import json
from typing import List, Dict

def parse_invoices(data: str) -> List[Dict]:
    ...
